title: Finding Regions of Interest
concept: Region proposals are candidate image regions identified by algorithms as highly likely to contain an object, reducing the search space.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Which parameter controls how aggressively NMS deletes overlapping boxes?
exercises.explain: The IoU threshold dictates the overlap percentage required to trigger suppression.

cards.title: Scanning a Map
cards.body: If you are looking for lost treasure in a massive desert, you don't dig up every single square inch of sand. You look at satellite images to find interesting rock formations or structures first. These are your candidate regions where treasure is likely to be.

cards.title: What are Region Proposals?
cards.body: Region Proposals (or Regions of Interest) are candidate bounding boxes generated by an algorithm that identifies regions in an image that are likely to contain an object, rather than background.

cards.title: Talent Scout
cards.body: Imagine a TV show talent search. A scout travels the country and picks 100 interesting acts (proposals). The main judges don't waste time watching everyone; they only evaluate the scout's selected candidates to pick the winner.

cards.title: Reducing the Search Space
cards.body: A 1000x1000 image has millions of possible sub-windows. A region proposal algorithm scans the image and outputs exactly 2,000 candidate boxes, ignoring empty sky or blank walls.

cards.title: They do not classify objects
cards.body: It's a myth that region proposal algorithms know what the object is. They are class-agnostic: they can tell you 'there is a blob that looks like an object here,' but they don't know if it's a cat, a car, or a sandwich.

cards.title: Brute Force vs. Proposals
cards.body: Comparing sliding window search vs region proposals:

left.text:
Test millions of windows:
Extremely slow search.

right.text:
Test only candidate boxes:
Focused, faster search.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary benefit of using region proposals in object detection?
exercises.explain: Region proposals focus the network's attention on candidate areas, eliminating the need to search millions of background windows.

options.text: It reduces the number of sub-windows the network needs to evaluate
It augments the training dataset features
It compresses the file size of the image

exercises.prompt: True or False: Region proposal algorithms are class-agnostic, meaning they locate candidate blobs without naming the category.
exercises.explain: True. Region proposal methods only look for 'objectness' (edges, textures) and leave classification to the neural network.
These candidate windows are referred to as Regions of Interest (RoIs).
exercises.sentence: Region proposal algorithms group pixels together to find candidate regions of ___.

exercises.prompt: Which process represents a region proposal step?
exercises.explain: Identifying candidate coordinates without labeling classes is region proposal.

options.text: Running a pixel grouping algorithm to output 2,000 candidate coordinates
Determining that a box contains a dog with 92% confidence

exercises.prompt: You are building a system to detect birds in a landscape photo. The photo is mostly empty blue sky, with a few birds in the center. How does a region proposal step help your classification network here?

options.text: It ignores the empty blue sky regions and only crops the bird-like shapes to send to the classifier.
It classifies the sky as background directly.
It increases the resolution of the birds.
It applies random horizontal flips to the sky.

exercises.explain: Exactly! By ignoring the sky and proposing only the bird regions, the network avoids wasting computation. Next: let's study the most famous classical proposal algorithm: Selective Search!
